project.title: Comparison of the rodlet layer proteins of Aspergillus flavus and Aspergillus fumiguatus
project.projectDescription: Atomic force microscopy (AFM) imaging showed that the rodlet layer of A. fumigatus and A. flavus was different.  The objective of this project was to examine the differences in the rodlet layer of these two pathogenic Aspergillus species.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Fifty milligram of Aspergillus condia was resuspended in 400 ul of formic acid and incubated in ice for 1 h for A. flavus and 2.5 h for A. fumigatus.  The formic acid extract was collected by centrifuging at 10,000 rpm for 10 min and the supernatant was dried under nitrogen gas stream.  The dried proteins were washed with deionized water and dried again before taking for MS analysis. The formic acid extracted proteins were resuspended in 1 M urea in 100 mM Ammonium bicarbonate and incubated at 37 deg for 15 mins. Proteins were reduced and alkylated and subjected to in-solution tryptic digestion.  The tryptic peptides were desalted using C18 columns and taken for analysis by nanoLC-Orbitrap Mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MS data was searched against the A. flavus NRRL3357, or A. fumigatus Af293 protein database using Proteome Discoverer software using Sequest and Mascot search engines. The database search was performed with the following parameters: peptide tolerance of 10 PPM and fragment tolerance of 0.60 Da to 0.80 Da, allowing two missed cleavages.  Cysteine carbamidomethylation was given as fixed modification while methionine oxidation, N-terminal acetylation and phosphorylation (S, T, Y) as variable modifications. The peptide spectrum matches (PSMs) from SequestHT and Mascot were post-processed using the Percolator algorithm (Käll, Lukas, et al 2005.). The peptides having a q-value lesser than the threshold of 0.01 were considered for protein identification.

references.referenceLine: Wong SSW, Venugopalan LP, Beaussart A, Karnam A, Mohammed MRS, Jayapal JM, Bretagne S, Bayry J, Prajna L, Kuppamuthu D, Latgé JP, Aimanianda V. Species-Specific Immunological Reactivities Depend on the Cell-Wall Organization of the Two Aspergillus, Aspergillus fumigatus and A. flavus. Front Cell Infect Microbiol. 2021 11:643312
